message:
fix: G.edges() returns live view for DiGraph/Multi* (br-r37-c1-msf5j)

fnx.DiGraph.edges(), fnx.MultiGraph.edges(), and fnx.MultiDiGraph.edges()
returned a frozen list snapshot at call time. nx returns a live view
(OutEdgeView / MultiEdgeDataView / OutMultiEdgeDataView) whose
iteration reflects subsequent graph mutations. Drop-in code that
captures a view and then mutates the graph silently broke under fnx.

Repro:
    >>> g = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
    >>> v = g.edges()
    >>> g.add_edge(3, 4)
    >>> list(v)
    [(1, 2), (2, 3)]            # nx returns [(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)]

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``:

- ``_DiGraphEdgeView.__call__``: when called with default args
  (``nbunch=None, data=False``), return ``self`` so the result is
  the same live view. Re-route ``__iter__`` to walk
  ``self._graph.succ`` directly instead of calling ``self()``,
  avoiding infinite recursion now that ``__call__`` can return self.
  Set-algebra dunders (``__or__`` / ``__and__`` / ``__sub__`` /
  ``__xor__``) updated to use ``self`` instead of ``self()``.

- ``_MultiGraphEdgeView.__call__`` /
  ``_MultiDiGraphEdgeView.__call__``: cannot return ``self`` because
  the no-paren ``G.edges`` view yields 3-tuples (with keys) but the
  with-paren ``G.edges()`` view must yield 2-tuples (without keys),
  matching nx's MultiEdgeView vs MultiEdgeDataView split. Introduce
  ``_LiveMultiEdgeCallView`` — a tiny wrapper that holds the graph
  and direction flag, re-walks ``adj`` / ``succ`` on every iteration
  (so it stays live), yields 2-tuples, and supports ``len`` /
  ``__contains__`` / set-eq.

- The data/keys/nbunch-filtered paths still snapshot to a list (a
  full ``EdgeDataView``-equivalent for those is out of scope here;
  they only break the live-view contract for explicitly-filtered
  callers, which is much rarer than ``g.edges()`` no-args).

Tests
-----
``tests/python/test_view_default_parity.py::TestEdgesCallReturnsLiveView``:
- test_edges_view_reflects_subsequent_add_edge
  (parametrized over Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph)
- test_edges_view_reflects_remove_edge
  (parametrized over the same four)
- test_multi_edges_call_yields_2tuples
  (parametrized over MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph; verifies the
  no-parens-yields-3-tuples vs with-parens-yields-2-tuples split)
- test_digraph_edges_view_len_matches: len() updates after add_edge
- test_multidigraph_edges_view_contains: ``in`` check tracks
  mutations, including the directed-only edge case

Full sweep
----------
6625 passed, 0 failed (was 6613 before — +12 new parametrized cases).

Note: a parallel defect exists for ``MultiGraph.nodes()`` /
``MultiDiGraph.nodes()`` (Rust-side ``MultiGraphNodeView`` /
``MultiDiGraphNodeView`` ``__call__`` returns list). That's a
separate scope (Rust-side change) and is not addressed here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -94,6 +94,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-m4msi","title":"parity: utils.random_sequence powerlaw_sequence + zipf_rv + cumulative_distribution + is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 functions)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced by franken_networkx: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). These are used by stochastic graph generators and degree-sequence sanity checks. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level and adding to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:59:39.857059179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:18:46.320103675Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:18:46.318235318Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution exposed at fnx top level matching nx.utils bit-for-bit when seeded; discrete_sequence on fnx.utils; is_valid_tree_degree_sequence is fnx-only ((bool, reason) tuple). Landed tests/python/test_random_sequence_parity.py (commit d2ed994) with 89 cases freezing the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mnrem","title":"parity: fnx.approximation.* exposed (G, *args, **kwargs) catch-all signatures, hiding all real kwargs","description":"_ApproximationNamespace dynamically wrapped each approximation function as (G, *args, **kwargs), so inspect.signature(fnx.approximation.X) returned a useless catch-all for every function. nx exposes the documented per-function signatures (average_clustering(G, trials, seed), christofides(G, weight, tree), greedy_tsp(G, weight, source), etc). 27 functions affected. Drop-in callers couldn't see what kwargs are accepted via introspection, IDEs and type-checkers couldn't help, and typos passed silently into nx's **kwargs handling. Fix: functools.wraps(nx_func) on the wrapper so the original signature/name/docstring are preserved. 30 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:41:51.191871760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.315185389Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.313339806Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 63a0163. _ApproximationNamespace.__getattr__ now uses functools.wraps so every fnx.approximation.X carries nx's exact signature, name, and docstring. 30 parity tests freeze the contract; all 27 previously-mismatched functions now pass introspection.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mpv5x","title":"parity: __str__ drift on view classes and Graph (name dropped, repr-style instead of bare-data)","description":"Several __str__ implementations diverge from nx: NodeView/EdgeView/DegreeView/NodeDataView/EdgeDataView/AtlasView/AdjacencyView fall back to __repr__ (e.g. 'NodeView((0, 1, 2))') instead of nx's bare-data form ('[0, 1, 2]'). Graph.__str__ ignores the graph name attribute: fnx returns 'Graph with 0 nodes and 0 edges' even when G.graph['name'] = 'G' is set; nx returns 'Graph named \'G\' with 0 nodes and 0 edges'. Drop-in code that does print(G), print(G.nodes), or asserts on str(view) gets different output. Fix: add explicit __str__ methods that return the bare-data forms matching nx exactly; fix Graph.__str__ to include the name when set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:36:59.610629652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.211977409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.209976079Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 73c840f. View __str__ returns bare data (list/dict) matching nx; Graph.__str__ includes name when set. 15 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-msf5j","title":"DiGraph/Multi*.edges() returns frozen list, not live view (vs nx OutEdgeView)","description":"fnx.DiGraph.edges(), fnx.MultiGraph.edges(), and fnx.MultiDiGraph.edges() return a frozen list snapshot at call time. nx returns a live view (OutEdgeView / MultiEdgeDataView / OutMultiEdgeDataView) that reflects subsequent graph mutations. Drop-in code that captures a view and then mutates the graph silently breaks under fnx.

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.DiGraph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> v = g.edges()
  >>> g.add_edge(3, 4)
  >>> list(v)
  [(1, 2), (2, 3)]            # nx returns [(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)]

Root cause: _DiGraphEdgeView.__call__ at python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:808 always builds and returns a list. Same pattern in _MultiGraphEdgeView.__call__ (returns _EdgeListWithSetAlgebra, a list subclass). nx's EdgeView.__call__(nbunch=None, data=False, default=None) returns self when called with default args.

Fix: short-circuit __call__ to return self when nbunch=None and data=False; keep current list-building behavior for the data=True/'attr'/nbunch-filtered paths (those are correctly modeled as snapshot views in nx via EdgeDataView, but matching that in full is out of scope here).

Note: a parallel defect exists for MultiGraph.nodes() / MultiDiGraph.nodes() (Rust-side NodeView class) — separate bead.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:33:07.797286806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:33:38.555359036Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nhz31","title":"test_coverage_gaps: refactor recent direct-nx wrappers + regenerate coverage.md","description":"Two pre-existing failures in test_coverage_gaps.py:
- test_public_coverage_has_no_networkx_delegated_exports: 54 exports classify as NX_DELEGATED. The test asserts == 0.
- test_generated_coverage_matrix_document_is_current: docs/coverage.md is out of date.

Most of the 54 NX_DELEGATED items pre-date my recent work. But my recent fixes added 6 more by directly importing nx in public functions:
- intersection (br-r37-c1-saf8a)
- k_truss (br-r37-c1-3qopf)
- maximum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- minimum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- transitive_closure_dag (br-r37-c1-utmy6)
- transitive_reduction (br-r37-c1-utmy6)

The project convention (used by stochastic_block_model, hoffman_singleton_graph, etc.) is to keep the nx import inside a private _xxx_impl helper so the public function is classified as PY_WRAPPER instead of NX_DELEGATED.

Scope of this bead: refactor the 6 I recently introduced to follow the convention, and regenerate docs/coverage.md. The other 48 pre-existing items are out of scope (separate refactor).

After this bead, the count drops from 54 → 48, the second test passes if docs are regenerated, and the first test still fails but no worse than before.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:53:43.443005498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.770550620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.768479207Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ntkdn","title":"parity: random_sequence powerlaw/zipf/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 fns)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). Used by stochastic generators and degree-seq sanity checks. Add wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:04:43.303897526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.064336059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.062433818Z","close_reason":"Shipped in b08418c: powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence wrappers; all 4 verified against nx (n=20 exp=2.5 seed=42 powerlaw match exactly, cumulative_distribution = [0,0.5,0.8,1.0]).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nzo8r","title":"random_regular_graph FailClosed for ~20% of seeds where nx succeeds","description":"fnx.random_regular_graph(d=4, n=10) raises ValueError(FailClosed{...}) for seeds 2,3,7,9,13,33,34,42,43,44 (10/50 = 20%) where nx succeeds. Root cause: crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs:670 uses a naive stub-pairing algorithm that throws away the entire attempt on any duplicate/self-edge and gives up after 100 attempts. nx's algorithm (see __init__.py:32986-33024) tracks unmatched stubs in potential_edges and only restarts on _suitable failure, which makes it converge in nearly all cases. Port nx's smarter inner loop into the Rust impl.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:23:16.888694411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:37:06.376266393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:37:06.373143578Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -786,8 +786,18 @@ class _DiGraphEdgeView:
     def __init__(self, graph):
         self._graph = graph
 
+    def _materialize(self):
+        result = []
+        for source in self._graph:
+            for target in self._graph.succ[source]:
+                result.append((source, target))
+        return result
+
     def __iter__(self):
-        return iter(self())
+        # br-r37-c1-msf5j: walk adj directly so __call__ can return
+        # self (a live view) without causing __iter__ → __call__
+        # recursion when called with default args.
+        return iter(self._materialize())
 
     def __len__(self):
         return self._graph.number_of_edges()
@@ -806,6 +816,18 @@ def __getitem__(self, edge):
         return self._graph.succ[u][v]
 
     def __call__(self, nbunch=None, data=False, default=None):
+        # br-r37-c1-msf5j: when called with default args, return self
+        # so the resulting "view" stays live with respect to subsequent
+        # graph mutations — matches nx's OutEdgeView.__call__ contract:
+        #
+        #   v = G.edges(); G.add_edge(...); list(v)  # sees the new edge
+        #
+        # The data/nbunch-filtered paths still snapshot to a list (a
+        # full EdgeDataView would be needed for live data views; out of
+        # scope here, and nx's EdgeDataView is also itself snapshot-ish
+        # in practice for many of its consumers).
+        if nbunch is None and data is False:
+            return self
         result = []
         for source in self._graph.nbunch_iter(nbunch):
             for target, attrs in self._graph.succ[source].items():
@@ -818,16 +840,16 @@ def __call__(self, nbunch=None, data=False, default=None):
         return result
 
     def __or__(self, other):
-        return set(self()) | set(other)
+        return set(self) | set(other)
 
     def __and__(self, other):
-        return set(self()) & set(other)
+        return set(self) & set(other)
 
     def __sub__(self, other):
-        return set(self()) - set(other)
+        return set(self) - set(other)
 
     def __xor__(self, other):
-        return set(self()) ^ set(other)
+        return set(self) ^ set(other)
 
 
 def _digraph_edges(self):
@@ -855,6 +877,77 @@ def _multi_edge_get(self, key, default=None):
         return default
 
 
+class _LiveMultiEdgeCallView:
+    """br-r37-c1-msf5j: result of ``MG.edges()`` (no args) on a (Multi)
+    graph. Mirrors nx's ``MultiEdgeDataView`` for the default-args
+    case: yields 2-tuples on iteration, stays live with respect to
+    subsequent graph mutations (re-walks ``adj`` / ``succ`` on every
+    iter), and supports ``len`` / ``in``. The ``MG.edges`` (no parens)
+    direct view continues to yield 3-tuples via ``_MultiGraphEdgeView``.
+    """
+
+    __slots__ = ("_graph", "_directed")
+
+    def __init__(self, graph, *, directed):
+        self._graph = graph
+        self._directed = directed
+
+    def _walk(self):
+        graph = self._graph
+        if self._directed:
+            for source in graph:
+                for target, keyed_attrs in graph.succ[source].items():
+                    for _key in keyed_attrs:
+                        yield (source, target)
+        else:
+            seen = set()
+            for source in graph:
+                for target, keyed_attrs in graph.adj[source].items():
+                    for _key in keyed_attrs:
+                        marker = (frozenset((source, target)), _key)
+                        if marker in seen:
+                            continue
+                        seen.add(marker)
+                        yield (source, target)
+
+    def __iter__(self):
+        return self._walk()
+
+    def __len__(self):
+        return self._graph.number_of_edges()
+
+    def __contains__(self, edge):
+        if not isinstance(edge, tuple) or len(edge) < 2:
+            return False
+        u, v = edge[0], edge[1]
+        graph = self._graph
+        if self._directed:
+            return u in graph.succ and v in graph.succ[u]
+        adj = graph.adj
+        return (u in adj and v in adj[u]) or (v in adj and u in adj[v])
+
+    def __repr__(self):
+        return f"MultiEdgeDataView({list(self)!r})"
+
+    def __eq__(self, other):
+        if isinstance(other, (set, frozenset)):
+            return set(self) == other
+        if isinstance(other, _LiveMultiEdgeCallView):
+            return list(self) == list(other)
+        try:
+            return list(self) == list(other)
+        except TypeError:
+            return NotImplemented
+
+    def __ne__(self, other):
+        eq = self.__eq__(other)
+        if eq is NotImplemented:
+            return NotImplemented
+        return not eq
+
+    __hash__ = None
+
+
 class _MultiGraphEdgeView:
     def __init__(self, graph):
         self._graph = graph
@@ -913,6 +1006,13 @@ def __contains__(self, edge):
         return False
 
     def __call__(self, nbunch=None, data=False, keys=False, default=None):
+        # br-r37-c1-msf5j: when called with all-default args, return a
+        # live wrapper that yields 2-tuples on iter and re-walks the
+        # graph each time so subsequent edge mutations are visible.
+        # Matches nx's MultiEdgeView.__call__() returning a live
+        # MultiEdgeDataView.
+        if nbunch is None and data is False and keys is False:
+            return _LiveMultiEdgeCallView(self._graph, directed=False)
         result = _EdgeListWithSetAlgebra()
         seen = set()
         for source in self._graph.nbunch_iter(nbunch):
@@ -1027,6 +1127,10 @@ def __getitem__(self, edge):
         raise KeyError(edge)
 
     def __call__(self, nbunch=None, data=False, keys=False, default=None):
+        # br-r37-c1-msf5j: live wrapper for default args (see
+        # _MultiGraphEdgeView.__call__).
+        if nbunch is None and data is False and keys is False:
+            return _LiveMultiEdgeCallView(self._graph, directed=True)
         result = _EdgeListWithSetAlgebra()
         for source in self._graph.nbunch_iter(nbunch):
             for target, keyed_attrs in self._graph.succ[source].items():

@@ -106,3 +106,90 @@ def test_digraph_edges_default(self):
         result = sorted(D.edges(data="capacity", default=0))
         nresult = sorted(nD.edges(data="capacity", default=0))
         assert result == nresult
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# br-r37-c1-msf5j: G.edges() must return a LIVE view (matches nx) for all
+# graph classes, not a frozen list snapshot. Subsequent edge mutations
+# should be visible when the captured view is iterated again.
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class TestEdgesCallReturnsLiveView:
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph", "MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+    def test_edges_view_reflects_subsequent_add_edge(self, cls_name):
+        fnx_cls = getattr(fnx, cls_name)
+        nx_cls = getattr(nx, cls_name)
+        G = fnx_cls([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+        Gn = nx_cls([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+
+        fview = G.edges()
+        nview = Gn.edges()
+
+        # Before mutation: same content
+        assert sorted(fview) == sorted(nview)
+
+        # Mutate
+        G.add_edge(3, 4)
+        Gn.add_edge(3, 4)
+
+        # The captured view must see the new edge in BOTH fnx and nx.
+        fnx_after = sorted(fview)
+        nx_after = sorted(nview)
+        assert fnx_after == nx_after, (
+            f"{cls_name}: live-view divergence — fnx={fnx_after} nx={nx_after}"
+        )
+        # Sanity: the new edge is visible.
+        if cls_name in ("Graph", "DiGraph"):
+            assert (3, 4) in fnx_after
+        else:
+            # Multi*: list contains 2-tuples (no key), so (3, 4) appears.
+            assert (3, 4) in fnx_after
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph", "MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+    def test_edges_view_reflects_remove_edge(self, cls_name):
+        fnx_cls = getattr(fnx, cls_name)
+        nx_cls = getattr(nx, cls_name)
+        G = fnx_cls([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+        Gn = nx_cls([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)])
+
+        fview = G.edges()
+        nview = Gn.edges()
+
+        G.remove_edge(2, 3)
+        Gn.remove_edge(2, 3)
+
+        assert sorted(fview) == sorted(nview)
+        # The removed edge is no longer visible in either fnx or nx.
+        for v in fview:
+            assert v[:2] != (2, 3)
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+    def test_multi_edges_call_yields_2tuples(self, cls_name):
+        """G.edges() (no args) on a multigraph yields 2-tuples,
+        matching nx's MultiEdgeDataView default. The MG.edges (no
+        parens) view continues to yield 3-tuples (with keys)."""
+        fnx_cls = getattr(fnx, cls_name)
+        nx_cls = getattr(nx, cls_name)
+        G = fnx_cls([(1, 2), (1, 2), (2, 3)])
+        Gn = nx_cls([(1, 2), (1, 2), (2, 3)])
+
+        # No-parens: yields 3-tuples
+        assert list(G.edges) == list(Gn.edges)
+        # With-parens: yields 2-tuples
+        assert list(G.edges()) == list(Gn.edges())
+
+    def test_digraph_edges_view_len_matches(self):
+        G = fnx.DiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+        v = G.edges()
+        assert len(v) == 2
+        G.add_edge(3, 4)
+        assert len(v) == 3
+
+    def test_multidigraph_edges_view_contains(self):
+        G = fnx.MultiDiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+        v = G.edges()
+        assert (1, 2) in v
+        assert (2, 1) not in v  # directed
+        G.add_edge(2, 1)
+        assert (2, 1) in v
